nuggets.headline: You might assume the artist behind Radiohead's often disquieting album art is as intense as the band's music, but their longtime collaborator has a surprisingly calm approach to his craft.
nuggets.text: Stanley Donwood, who has created all of Radiohead's album art for 25 years, is known for his frequently frightening and unsettling visuals, including the bold, yet uneasy, sleeve for *In Rainbows*. Despite the dark nature of his art, Donwood himself is described as calming and very funny, advocating for regular breaks to 'watch the paint dry' during his creative process.

source.title: Stanley Donwood Reveals What it's Really Like Working With Radiohead for 25 Years - ELEPHANT
source.quoteSnippet: But Donwood himself is calming and very funny, in that particularly dry, English way. You can sense this from his recent book, There Will Be No Quiet.

nuggets.headline: Before it became a beloved studio track, 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi' made its public debut in a surprisingly grand and unusual setting, years before *In Rainbows* was even released.
nuggets.text: Thom Yorke and Jonny Greenwood first premiered 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi' in March 2005 at London's Ether Festival, performing it live not just as a duo, but accompanied by the Nazareth Orchestra. This orchestral premiere gave early audiences a taste of the song's intricate beauty long before its official release on *In Rainbows* in 2007.

source.title: Weird Fishes / Arpeggi by Radiohead - Songfacts
source.quoteSnippet: Thom Yorke and Jonny Greenwood from Radiohead together with the Nazareth Orchestra premiered this song at the Ether Festival (Royal Festival Hall, London) on March 27, 2005.

nuggets.headline: If the epic scope and soaring, intricate melodies of 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi' left you wanting more, you absolutely need to explore the dramatic soundscapes of Muse.
nuggets.text: Muse, often categorized within the same art rock and alternative rock spheres as Radiohead, excels at crafting expansive, emotionally resonant soundscapes. Their music frequently features powerful, high-pitched vocals, elaborate instrumental arrangements, and a blend of melancholic beauty with moments of intense, almost orchestral, grandeur, echoing the textural depth and progressive elements found in 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi.'

source.title: Thom Yorke
source.quoteSnippet: Yorke's early influences included alternative rock acts such as the Pixies and R.E.M.

artistSummary: Radiohead is an influential English rock band, fronted by Thom Yorke, celebrated for their constantly evolving soundscapes from alternative rock to electronic music. With over 30 million albums sold, they are known for profound lyrics, intricate arrangements, and a consistent collaboration with visual artist Stanley Donwood. Their work often explores themes of modern anxiety and the digital age's impact.
